exemplar.title: Is bias inevitable in the production of knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: Is bias inevitable in the production of knowledge?

strengths: Three specific, well-situated real-world objects are clearly identified and contextualized.
Links between each object and the IA prompt are coherent, sustained and supported by concrete evidence.
Most points are backed up by explicit references to sources, demonstrating purposeful selection and good command of the material.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into broad TOK generalizations without sufficient evidence or citation.
Bibliographic and in-text citation formatting is inconsistent, and key data points (e.g., sample sizes, timestamps) are sometimes missing.
Limited engagement with counter-arguments or alternative perspectives reduces the depth of critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition excels at grounding each object in a precise context and sustaining clear, well-evidenced links to the IA prompt, pushing it into the top band. While the student demonstrates strong real-world insights and purposeful object selection, minor lapsesâsuch as unsupported generalizations, inconsistent referencing, and a lack of counter-argument considerationâprevent the work from achieving a perfect 10. Awarding 9 reflects its clear alignment with the 9â10 band descriptor while acknowledging these remaining areas for improvement.

annotations.comment: The IA prompt is clearly stated and directly frames the exploration of bias, providing a strong foundation for linking each object back to the central question.
The table for Object 1 is disorganized and lacks clear column labels or definitions, making it difficult to interpret the data and its relevance to algorithmic bias.
The student explicitly identifies a specific frame and sourceâJoy Buolamwiniâs 2018 poemâthereby situating the object in its real-world context as required.
To strengthen the analysis, include specific data points (e.g., video timestamp, sample size of misclassifications) to ground the example in concrete evidence.
The title of Object 3 precisely names the article and author, fulfilling the requirement to situate the example in its exact real-world context.
The explanation merges general tool limitations with algorithmic bias; clarify the distinction between physical constraints and biases embedded in training data or code.
The appeal to utilitarian vs deontological ethics is asserted without a clear link to the object; explicitly connect these philosophical frameworks to the Amazon Rekognition example.
Consider addressing counterargumentsâsuch as successful bias mitigation in facial recognitionâto deepen the critical evaluation of algorithmic inevitability.
The pre- and post-lobotomy photographs effectively anchor the discussion in a real historical context, illustrating value-driven bias in medical practice.
The description generalizes the use of lobotomies without citing prevalence data or primary sources; include statistics or historical records to support these claims.
Expand on how confirmation bias influenced specific diagnostic criteria in the Dully caseâciting case notes or academic analyses would improve precision.
The claim that value-based confirmation bias is unconscious lacks scholarly support; reference cognitive-bias research to validate or nuance this assertion.
To provide balance, discuss historical attempts (if any) to mitigate value-based bias in psychiatry, such as evolving diagnostic manuals.
How does the Dully case illustrate that bias might be mitigated through different social or institutional values rather than being entirely inevitable?
Including the articleâs published page scan grounds the studentâs analysis in a tangible source, enhancing the authenticity of the real-world object.
The study summary is too general; cite key figures such as the number of unpublished trials or effect-size differences to substantiate the discussion of publication bias.
Explicitly link the Turner et al. findings back to the IA prompt by discussing how power dynamics render certain results publishable or suppressible.
The discussion references realist thinkers without citations; integrating TOK theory sources would strengthen the philosophical dimension of the argument.
Consider discussing reforms such as mandatory clinical trial registries as counterexamples, showing that publication bias can be reduced.
Could the analysis explore whether open-access mandates or data-sharing initiatives demonstrate that bias in publication is not wholly inevitable?
Citation entries are inconsistent in format; select and apply a single referencing style across all sources for clarity.
The Medium citation redundantly lists 'Medium' twice; streamline the entry to include only one publisher and ensure accurate date formatting.
A URL is split across two lines, hindering verification; present full URLs on one line or prefer DOI links to maintain accessibility.
The Forbes citation entry is truncated mid-title; include the complete article title and access date to ensure full attribution.
Add access dates for all online sources and consider alphabetizing the bibliography by author surname to improve consistency and organization.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
